title: The Fox and the Hedgehog
lines: A Fox and a Hedgehog were walking together.
"I have a hundred tricks to escape from dogs," boasted the Fox.
"I have but one," said the Hedgehog.
When they encountered a pack of hunting dogs, the Fox tried one trick after another, but the dogs would not be fooled.
The Hedgehog simply rolled into a ball and was safe from the dogs.
"Your one trick seems to be worth more than my hundred," said the Fox.
moral: One good strategy is better than many uncertain ones.
